question: Among the English tweets, how many have a likes count of under 5? lang = 'en' stands for English tweets
orig_question: Among the weekly issuance accounts, how many have a loan of under 200000? frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE' stands for weekly issuance
orig_masked: Among the [C4], how many have a [T6] of under [V2]? [C4] = '[V1]' stands for [C4]; [V1] is a value of the column [C4]; [V2] is a value of the column [C34]
orig_evidence: frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE' stands for weekly issuance
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.tweetid) FROM twitter AS T1 WHERE T1.lang = 'en' AND T1.likes < 5
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.account_id) FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T2.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE' AND T1.amount < 200000

question: What is the average billable period of all hispanic patients?  hispanic refers to race = 'hispanic'; average billable period refers to AVG(total)
orig_question: What is the average weight of all female superheroes?  female refers to gender = 'Female'; average weight refers to AVG(weight_kg)
orig_masked: What is the average [C29] of all [V1] [T9]?  [V1] refers to [C7] = '[V1]'; average [C29] refers to AVG([C29]); [V1] is a value of the column [C7]
evidence: hispanic refers to race = 'hispanic'; average billable period refers to AVG(total)
orig_evidence: female refers to gender = 'Female'; average weight refers to AVG(weight_kg)
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(T1.total) FROM claims AS T1 INNER JOIN patients AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.patient WHERE T2.race = 'hispanic'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT AVG(T1.weight_kg) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN gender AS T2 ON T1.gender_id = T2.id WHERE T2.gender = 'Female'

question: What is the most common marital status of customers with farming or fishing occupation? the most common marital status refers to MAX(marital_status); 'farming or fishing' refers to occupation
orig_question: What is the most common sign of patients with SLE disease? the most common sign refers to MAX(symptoms); 'SLE' refers to diagnosis
orig_masked: What is the most common [C12] of [T1] with [V1] [C6]? the most common [C12] refers to MAX([C12]); '[V1]' refers to [C6]; [V1] is a value of the column [C6]
evidence: the most common marital status refers to MAX(marital_status); 'farming or fishing' refers to occupation
orig_evidence: the most common sign refers to MAX(symptoms); 'SLE' refers to diagnosis
gold_sql: SELECT T1.marital_status FROM customers AS T1 WHERE T1.occupation = 'Farming-fishing' GROUP BY T1.marital_status ORDER BY COUNT(T1.marital_status) DESC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT Symptoms FROM Examination WHERE Diagnosis = 'SLE' GROUP BY Symptoms ORDER BY COUNT(Symptoms) DESC LIMIT 1

question: For customers with education level lower than 2, list their age, sex and average income. education level lower than 2 refers to educationnum < 2;
orig_question: For patient with albumin level lower than 3.5, list their ID, sex and diagnosis. albumin level lower than 3.5 refers to ALB < 3.5;
orig_masked: For [T3] with [C14] lower than [V1], list their [C63], [C64] and [C61]. [C14] lower than [V1] refers to [C14] < [V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C14]
evidence: education level lower than 2 refers to educationnum < 2;
orig_evidence: albumin level lower than 3.5 refers to ALB < 3.5;
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.age, T1.sex, T2.income_k FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN demog AS T2 ON T1.geoid = T2.geoid WHERE T1.educationnum < 2
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.ID, T1.SEX, T1.Diagnosis FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.ALB < 3.5

question: Which employee is more senior, Manin or Foster? The larger the hire date value, the more senior the person is, and vice versa;
orig_question: Which player is older, Aaron Lennon or Abdelaziz Barrada? The larger the birthday value, the younger the person is, and vice versa;
orig_masked: Which [T4] is [C121], [V2] or [V1]? The larger the [C121] value, the [C121] the [T4] is, and vice versa;; [V1] is a value of the column [C126]; [V2] is a value of the column [C126]
evidence: The larger the hire date value, the younger the person is, and vice versa;
orig_evidence: The larger the birthday value, the younger the person is, and vice versa;
gold_sql: SELECT T1.lastname FROM employee AS T1 WHERE T1.lastname IN ('Manin', 'Foster') ORDER BY T1.hiredate ASC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT player_name FROM Player WHERE player_name IN ('Aaron Lennon', 'Abdelaziz Barrada') ORDER BY birthday ASC LIMIT 1

question: How many high-level apps have "SOCIAL" category of app?  High-level apps refers to the apps with the 1,000,000,000+ installs.
orig_question: How many high-level credit cards have "OWNER" type of disposition?  High-level credit cards refers to the cards with the gold type.
orig_masked: How many [C8] [T2] have "[V1]" [C8] of [T4]?  [C8] [T2] refers to the [T2] with the [V2] [C8].; [V1] is a value of the column [C16]; [V2] is a value of the column [C8]
evidence: High-level apps refers to the apps with the 1,000,000,000+ installs.
orig_evidence: High-level credit cards refers to the cards with the gold type.
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.app) FROM playstore AS T1 INNER JOIN playstore AS T2 ON T1.app = T2.app WHERE T1.installs = '1,000,000,000+' AND T2.category = 'SOCIAL'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.card_id) FROM card AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.disp_id = T2.disp_id WHERE T1.type = 'gold' AND T2.type = 'OWNER'

question: What is the code of the immunization MMR in year 2008? in year 2008 refers to strftime('%Y', date) = '2008';
orig_question: What is the overall rating of the football player Gabriel Tamas in year 2011? in year 2011 refers to strftime('%Y', date) = '2011';
orig_masked: What is the [C153] of the [T4] [V1] in [C136] [V2]? in [C136] [V2] refers to strftime('%Y', [C136]) = '[V2]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C126]; [V2] is a value of the column [C136]
evidence: in year 2008 refers to strftime('%Y', date) = '2008';
orig_evidence: in year 2011 refers to strftime('%Y', date) = '2011';
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.code FROM immunizations AS T1 INNER JOIN immunizations AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.patient AND T1.encounter = T2.encounter AND T1.date = T2.date WHERE T1.description = 'MMR' AND strftime('%Y', T2.date) = '2008' LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t2.overall_rating FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id WHERE t1.player_name = 'Gabriel Tamas' AND strftime('%Y', t2.date) = '2011'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: List the loan ID, district and average salary for loan with duration of 60 months.  A3 refers to regions; A11 refers to average salary
orig_masked: List the [C37], [T5] and [C18] for [T6] with [C36] of [V1] [C36].  [T5] refers to [T5]; [C18] refers to [C18]; [V1] is a value of the column [C36]
evidence: abbreviation refers to state; avg_income_per_household refers to average income per household
orig_evidence: A3 refers to regions; A11 refers to average salary
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T3.loan_id, T2.A2, T2.A11 FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN loan AS T3 ON T1.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.duration = 60

question: How many episodes have the award of "Primetime Emmy"?  award of "Primetime Emmy" refers to award_category = 'Primetime Emmy'
orig_question: How many superheroes have the super power of "Super Strength"?  super power of "Super Strength" refers to power_name = 'Super Strength'
orig_masked: How many [C12] have the [T10] of "[V1]"?  [T10] of "[V1]" refers to [C31] = '[V1]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C31]
evidence: award of "Primetime Emmy" refers to award_category = 'Primetime Emmy'
orig_evidence: super power of "Super Strength" refers to power_name = 'Super Strength'
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT episode_id) FROM award WHERE award_category = 'Primetime Emmy'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.hero_id) FROM hero_power AS T1 INNER JOIN superpower AS T2 ON T1.power_id = T2.id WHERE T2.power_name = 'Super Strength'

question: Provide the voice actor and movie_title of Chernabog. voice actor refers to voice-actor; Chernabog is the villain of characters;
orig_question: Provide the hero name and race of Charles Chandler. hero name refers to superhero_name; Charles Chandler is the full name of superhero;
orig_masked: Provide the [C28] and [C17] of [V1]. [C28] refers to [C28]; [V1] is the [C20] of [T9];; [V1] is a value of the column [C20]
evidence: voice actor refers to voice-actor; Chernabog is the villain of characters;
orig_evidence: hero name refers to superhero_name; Charles Chandler is the full name of superhero;
gold_sql: SELECT T1."voice-actor", T2.movie_title FROM "voice-actors" AS T1 INNER JOIN characters AS T2 ON T1.movie = T2.movie_title WHERE T1.character = 'Chernabog'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.superhero_name, T2.race FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.race_id = T2.id WHERE T1.full_name = 'Charles Chandler'
